title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 12: Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 47 + 28 - 15?
A rectangle has a width of 4 cm and a length of 9 cm. What is its perimeter in centimetres?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 70' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='15' y='15' width='90' height='40'/><text x='60' y='10' text-anchor='middle' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='10'>9 cm</text><text x='110' y='38' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='10'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labelled 9 cm on top and 4 cm on the right side.

questions.question_text: A train leaves the station at 9:35 and travels for 50 minutes. At what time does it arrive?
The pattern starts 4, 9, 14, 19, ... What is the seventh term?
A bottle contains 1.5 litres of juice. Mark pours out 350 millilitres. How much juice remains in the bottle?
How many small squares make up the shaded L-shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.3'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.3'/><rect x='10' y='50' width='20' height='20' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.3'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='20' height='20' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.3'/><rect x='30' y='70' width='20' height='20' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.3'/><rect x='50' y='70' width='20' height='20' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.3'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shape made of unit squares: four stacked vertically on the left and two extending to the right at the bottom.

questions.question_text: Lara has three cards showing the digits 2, 5, and 7. She arranges them to form the smallest possible three-digit number. What is it?
questions.solution: Smallest arrangement places smallest digit first: 257.

questions.question_text: What is 6 multiplied by 13, then divided by 2?
A triangle has angles of 40 degrees and 75 degrees. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 80' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='20,65 100,65 55,15'/><text x='28' y='60' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='9'>40°</text><text x='85' y='60' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='9'>75°</text><text x='50' y='28' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='9'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle with base angles labelled 40 degrees and 75 degrees, and the top angle marked with a question mark.

questions.question_text: Which of the following numbers is a multiple of both 3 and 4?
questions.solution: A multiple of both 3 and 4 must be a multiple of 12. Only 24 qualifies.

questions.question_text: In a bag of marbles, the ratio of red to blue is 2 to 5. If there are 14 red marbles, how many blue marbles are there?
questions.solution: 14 red means 7 groups of 2, so blue = 7 * 5 = 35.

questions.question_text: The figure below is made of three identical squares. If the total perimeter of the figure is 32 cm, what is the side length of one square?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 60' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='90' y='10' width='40' height='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three congruent squares placed side by side in a row to form a long rectangle.
questions.solution: The combined shape is a rectangle of sides 3s and s, perimeter = 8s = 32, so s = 4 cm.

questions.question_text: Two identical pencils and one eraser cost 85 cents. If one pencil costs 30 cents, how much does the eraser cost?
Priya has 4 different T-shirts and 3 different pairs of shorts. How many distinct outfits pairing one T-shirt with one pair of shorts can she wear?
The letters K A N G A R O O are written in order and repeat endlessly: K A N G A R O O K A N G A R O O ... What letter is in the 50th position?
questions.solution: The pattern has length 8. 50 / 8 = 6 remainder 2. The 2nd letter is A.

questions.question_text: Four friends finished a race. Omar finished before Lee. Rita finished after Lee but before Sam. Who finished last?
questions.solution: Order: Omar, Lee, Rita, Sam. Sam finished last.

questions.question_text: A square of side 10 cm has a smaller square of side 4 cm cut out from one corner. What is the area of the remaining shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><path d='M 10 10 L 110 10 L 110 110 L 10 110 Z M 110 10 L 70 10 L 70 50 L 110 50' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.15'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='40' height='40' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 10 by 10 square with a 4 by 4 square removed from the top-right corner, leaving an L-shape.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of the smallest two-digit prime number and the largest two-digit prime number?
questions.solution: Smallest two-digit prime is 11; largest is 97. 11 + 97 = 108.

questions.question_text: A rope is 6.4 metres long. It is cut into pieces each 80 centimetres long. How many pieces are made?
The average of four numbers is 21. Three of them are 15, 22, and 28. What is the fourth number?

On an island, every inhabitant is either a knight (always truthful) or a knave (always lying). Three inhabitants P, Q, and R are asked whether they are knights. P says "I am a knight." Q says "P is lying." R says "Q is lying." How many knights are among them?
questions.solution: P's statement is consistent whether P is knight or knave, so P's type is unknown; thus Q and R each mirror opposite states but P's role cannot be pinned down.

questions.question_text: A cube is painted on all faces and then cut into 27 identical small cubes. How many of the small cubes have exactly two faces painted?
questions.image_description: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube drawn in perspective, showing the grid of small cubes on visible faces.
questions.solution: Cubes with exactly two faces painted lie along edges but not at corners. A 3x3x3 cube has 12 such edge-cubes.

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is 48, and their difference is 14. What is the smaller number?
How many three-digit numbers have all digits odd?

In the figure, two congruent equilateral triangles overlap to form a six-pointed star. If each small triangular point has an area of 2 cm squared, and the inner hexagon has an area of 12 cm squared, what is the total area of the star?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 100,90 20,90'/><polygon points='60,110 100,30 20,30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A six-pointed star formed by overlapping two equilateral triangles, with a regular hexagon in the middle.
questions.solution: Star area = inner hexagon + 6 small points = 12 + 6 * 2 = 24 cm squared.

questions.question_text: What is the remainder when 7 raised to the 20th power is divided by 10?
questions.solution: Last digits of powers of 7 cycle: 7, 9, 3, 1 every 4 powers. 20 is a multiple of 4, so last digit is 1.

questions.question_text: A recipe uses flour and sugar in a 5 to 2 ratio. If the baker uses a total of 910 grams of flour and sugar, how many grams of sugar are used?
questions.solution: Total parts = 7. One part = 910 / 7 = 130 g. Sugar = 2 * 130 = 260 g.

questions.question_text: In a row of lamps numbered 1 through 100, every lamp starts off. First, you flip the switch on every 2nd lamp; then every 3rd lamp; then every 5th lamp. After all three passes, how many lamps are on?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='8'/><circle cx='50' cy='20' r='8' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.4'/><circle cx='80' cy='20' r='8' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.4'/><circle cx='110' cy='20' r='8'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='8' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='0.4'/><circle cx='170' cy='20' r='8'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of circular lamps, some shaded to suggest the on-off toggling pattern.
questions.solution: A lamp ends on if it was flipped an odd number of times. Using inclusion-exclusion on divisibility by 2, 3, 5 up to 100 gives 43 lamps switched an odd number of times.

questions.question_text: A digital clock shows the time in the format HH:MM. How many times in a 24-hour day do all four digits displayed differ from each other?
questions.solution: Counting valid HH:MM (00:00 to 23:59) with four distinct digits gives 588.

questions.question_text: A regular hexagon is divided into 6 equilateral triangles by drawing all its long diagonals. If the hexagon has a total area of 54 cm squared, what is the area of one triangle?
questions.image_description: A regular hexagon divided into six equilateral triangles by its three long diagonals meeting at the centre.
